summary: The COVID-19 pandemic started suddenly for me in early 2020, and I remember the fear and uncertainty when we had to start working from home. I caught COVID in December 2020 after attending my nephew's funeral, and sadly, a close friend named Vicki Whitfill also got sick and later passed away. I experienced severe symptoms and long-term health issues, including swollen joints and mobility problems, which have lasted over two years. I also got vaccinated in early 2021, but had serious reactions that affected my health significantly.

I went to Albany, Georgia, to get my COVID vaccine at a military base there in February 2021. The place was packed, and although I was hesitant at first, my aunt encouraged me to get the shot. The drive back from Albany to my parents' home took about an hour and a half, and soon after the vaccination, I started experiencing severe side effects including a blistering rash and joint pain.

After my first COVID vaccine shot, I developed a blistering rash on my injection arm that was sore and oozing. To manage the symptoms, I used Benadryl which helped with the soreness and blistering. It was part of my early treatment as I dealt with the vaccine reaction in February 2021.

I got my second COVID vaccine shot at the Delta Museum around late March or early April 2021. Before taking the second shot, I informed the staff about my reaction to the first dose, and after checking with my doctor, they cleared me to proceed. Unfortunately, the second shot caused me to have a high fever and severe joint pain that left me unable to walk for a couple of days.

When the pandemic started shutting things down in March 2020, I was worried about groceries and essentials. Luckily, I was a Sam's Club member, so I already had tissue and water stocked up, which helped me get through the initial panic. It was reassuring to have those supplies while stores were scrambling to keep items like Lysol and Clorox available.

During the early days of the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020, I turned to Amazon to try and find cleaning supplies like Lysol and other sanitizers since stores were out of stock or charging exorbitant prices. Amazon was one of the few places where I could attempt to keep things sanitary while navigating shortages. It was part of how I tried to maintain safety for myself and my family.

At the start of the pandemic in March 2020, Clorox products were nearly impossible to find due to high demand. My dad, who is a chemical engineer, helped me mix a homemade cleaning solution to keep us germ-free until we could get actual Clorox. This DIY approach was essential for us while stores were sold out and prices were inflated.

My nephew was tragically killed by an Atlanta Police Department officer about two years ago. It happened after an incident at a club in Atlanta where he was defending his girlfriend from a security guard. The situation escalated quickly, resulting in both my nephew and the APD officer shooting each other. The investigation is still ongoing, and it has deeply affected my family.

Atlanta is where my nephew was killed, which was a pivotal and painful moment for my family. The incident occurred at a club in the city, and it was also where I attended the funeral in December 2020 during which I contracted COVID. Atlanta has been central to some of the most difficult experiences I've faced over the past few years.

Vicki Whitfill was a high school friend and a nurse who came to support me at my nephew's funeral in December 2020. Unfortunately, she caught COVID around the same time I did, and we kept in touch daily discussing our symptoms. Sadly, Vicki passed away from the virus, which was a heartbreaking loss and made the reality of the pandemic very personal for me.
